ui.ae.add_insert_slot_tt: Add another insert row (fuzzy search from the plugin scan).
ui.ae.apply_inserts_tt: Load the selected plug-ins for library playback. Stop the output stream first.
ui.ae.audio_device_type_tt: JUCE audio device type (CoreAudio, ASIO, JACK, etc.). Changing driver stops active streams and refreshes device lists.
ui.ae.buffer_frames_input_tt: Optional fixed buffer for start_input_stream. The list comes from the selected input device‚Äôs supported sizes (get_input_device_info.buffer_sizes). Choose Driver default to omit buffer_frames.
ui.ae.buffer_frames_output_tt: Optional fixed buffer for start_output_stream. The list comes from the selected output device‚Äôs supported sizes (get_output_device_info.buffer_sizes). Choose Driver default to omit buffer_frames.
ui.ae.err_no_ipc: Audio engine IPC unavailable (not running in the app shell).
ui.ae.heading: Audio Engine
ui.ae.input_device_tt: Choose which input is used for the caps readout and for Start input capture. Live capture status and peak update in the engine status card.
ui.ae.input_devices_intro: Choose an input, view driver caps, and start or stop capture. The list comes from list_input_devices; live capture status and peak update in the engine status card.
ui.ae.insert_editor_tt: Open the native plug-in window for this insert slot. Apply inserts first.
ui.ae.insert_slot_tt: Optional VST3 or AU effect (from scan). Order is serial: slot 1 ‚Üí slot 4.
ui.ae.intro: Low-latency JUCE AudioEngine: pick driver (device type), optional sample rate, then output device ‚Äî Apply opens an output stream (silence or optional 440 Hz test tone on F32 devices). Input section can start a capture stream (samples discarded until metering/DSP). engine_state returns output `stream` and `input_stream`. Optional buffer_frames applies to both directions. Playback output & EQ mirror the floating player; VST3/AU inserts run after the built-in EQ when you Apply inserts (stop the output stream first).
ui.ae.output_device_tt: System output device for the audio engine
ui.ae.playback_dsp_intro: Same settings as the floating player (saved in preferences). Applies to library playback through the AudioEngine when a track is playing.
ui.ae.playback_transport_intro: Rodio transport: play/pause, seek ¬±5s, reverse (same behavior as the floating player).
ui.ae.plugin_scan_cache_desc: Per-module timeout for the JUCE scan subprocess. Wipe deletes cache files and starts a full rescan.
ui.ae.plugin_scan_timeout_tt: Seconds per plug-in module before the scan worker kills the child process (5‚Äì3600). Saved in preferences as pluginScanTimeoutSec.
ui.ae.plugins_insert_intro: Pick up to four effects from the scanned plug-in list. Stop output stream, Apply inserts, then play from the library.
ui.ae.plugins_scan_stuck_hint: ‚Äî Stalled: JUCE may be hung inside this plug-in (not the same as a crash). Restart Audio Engine AudioEngine if this never advances.
ui.ae.plugins_scanning_note: Scanning VST3/AU plug-ins in the background (bad modules are skipped).
ui.ae.plugins_stub: Phase: {phase}. Planned formats: {formats}. Active insert slots: {count}. {note}
ui.ae.process_stats_not_running: Subprocesul AudioEngine nu ruleazƒÉ √ÆncƒÉ (pornit la primul IPC).
ui.ae.restart_audioengine_tt: Kill the audio-engine process and spawn a fresh one if it crashed or stopped responding. AudioEngine stream and playback state is reset.
ui.ae.sample_rate_tt: Optional fixed sample rate for start_output_stream and start_input_stream. Options come from the selected output device; leave as driver default if unsure.
ui.ae.start_input_capture_tt: Open cpal input stream (samples discarded until metering/DSP)
ui.ae.status_applied_stream: Device {id} saved; silence output stream running.
ui.ae.status_row_desc: Versiune AudioEngine, fluxuri, capturƒÉ intrare »ôi CPU/memorie/fire/FD-uri subproces (doar citire; acelea»ôi sonde ca √Æn antetul aplica»õiei).
ui.ae.status_stub: Not connected ‚Äî engine process not implemented yet.
ui.ae.stop_stream_tt: Stop the cpal output stream (silence placeholder)
ui.ae.test_tone_tt: Low-level sine on F32 default stream; toggle live if supported
ui.ae.transport_reverse_tt: Reverse playback ‚Äî full decode in AudioEngine RAM when enabled (same as floating player)
ui.ae.transport_section_desc: Play/pause, seek ¬±5s, reverse ‚Äî same as the floating player.
ui.ae.wipe_rescan_plugins_tt: Delete known-plugin-list.xml and related cache files, then run a full VST3/AU scan (uses the timeout above).

ui.sd.app_log_verbosity_desc: Normal: scan lifecycle, errors, TCC/readdir failures, incremental warnings. Verbose: SMB dedup/retry/enter traces, MIDI mount/dedup, each live KVR fetch, incremental snapshot key count, entry counts per directory at depth 0‚Äì2. Quiet: optional prefix filter on normal lines (none by default).

ui.sd.ipc_buffer_capacity_between_scan_t: IPC buffer capacity between scan threads and UI. Higher = less blocking. Restart required.‚Äå
ui.sd.items_per_batch_sent_from_scanner_: Items per batch sent from scanner to UI. Larger = fewer IPC calls. Restart required.‚Äå

ui.sd.max_open_files_per_process_raise_i: Max open files per process. Raise if scans abort on large libraries or network shares. Restart required.‚Äå
ui.sd.minimum_ms_between_dom_updates_dur: Minimum ms between DOM updates during scans (lower = smoother, higher = faster)‚Äå

ui.sd.prune_old_scans: On startup (after a delay), remove older completed scan runs except the last N per scanner type. Turn off to keep all history (larger DB). Next launch applies.
ui.sd.prune_old_scans_keep: Number of most recent completed scans retained per type (plugins, samples, DAW, presets, MIDI, PDF) when pruning is on.

ui.sd.reset_all_sizing_positioning_order: Reset ALL sizing, positioning, ordering, and layout to factory defaults. Clears tab order, column order/widths, section order, modal positions, player dock, stat bar order, toolbar order, dashboard card order, and all drag-reorder prefs. Requires reload.‚Äå

ui.sd.show_the_audio_player_when_the_app: Show the audio player when the app starts (restores last playing state)‚Äå
ui.sd.sqlite_read_pool_extra: Extra read-only database connections for parallel queries (WAL). 0 = automatic (CPU-based, capped). Higher uses more RAM. Restart required.
